rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in valid component type scopes; avoid implementation-level bindings in type annexes unless using visible outputs.
Use only visible identifiers and types from the current component scope; do not introduce package-qualified or undefined references.
generic_rules: Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and use only declared or visible variables.
AGREE contracts should use logical operators and valid equation declarations with matching types.
Component type contracts must reference only visible features; implementation-level guarantees should be in implementation scopes.
Avoid introducing undefined or package-qualified identifiers in AGREE annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks with valid syntax like `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const`.
Helper functions and equations must be declared with correct types and used only with visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings or subcomponent references in component type annexes.
Do not introduce undefined or package-qualified identifiers that are not visible in the current AADL scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
Model fusion or component insertion locations
